records.ety: [Cf. Scot. grome, groyme, grume, gome, guym, man, lover, OD. grom boy, youth; perh. the r is an insertion as in E. bridegroom, and the word is the same as AS. guma man. See Bridegroom.]

defs: A boy or young man; a waiter; a servant; especially, a man or boy who has charge of horses, or the stable.
One of several officers of the English royal household, chiefly in the lord chamberlain's department; as, the groom of the chamber; the groom of the stole.
A man recently married, or about to be married; a bridegroom.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Groomed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Grooming.]

defs: To tend or care for, or to curry or clean, as a, horse.
